summary: PR 2527 split the tail of `src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.c` into ten new fighter-common translation units from `ftCo_800C703C.c` through `ftCo_800C7CA0.c`, added matching headers and build/split metadata, and updated call sites/includes across fighter and item code. The bot report shows net code/data progress and 55 new matches, while also reporting old `ft_0C31` matches as broken because symbols moved to the new units.

key_files.role: Original fighter source shortened; functions from 0x800C703C onward removed into new per-address units while local `fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate` comments/asserts were adjusted.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ft_0C31.c removes function bodies from ftCo_800C703C through ft_800C80A4
raw_diff: comments on fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate preserving jobj.h file/line asserts while calling fighter-local dirty helper

key_files.role: Original header reduced after moving prototypes to new per-address headers.
New split unit for `ftCo_800C703C`.
New split unit for rebirth-wait helper and inline wrapper chain.
New split unit for Mario/Luigi wait path helpers.
New split unit for Mario/Luigi walk-slow path helpers.
New split unit for Kirby wait/walk helpers.
New split unit for Kirby walk-fast helper.
New split unit for Giga Koopa and Leadead capture trigger helpers.
New split unit for Leadead capture state and callbacks.
New split unit for Likelike capture state and collision/damage helpers.
New split unit for down-reflect state, wall collision reflection helper, material object global, and `ft_800C80A4` loop.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: uses `PAD_STACK`, goto-marked branch structure, and for/while loops over fighter parts/dobjs

Split metadata updated to end `ft_0C31.c` at 0x800C703C and add the ten new text/sdata2 ranges; sbss ownership split between `ftCo_800C7CA0.c` and `ft_0C88.c`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `ft_0C31.c` text range changed from 0x800C61B0-0x800C8170 to 0x800C61B0-0x800C703C
raw_diff: new split ranges for `ftCo_800C703C.c` through `ftCo_800C7CA0.c`

key_files.role: Symbol metadata updated for local/global names, sdata2 literals, and sbss object sizes/scopes affected by the split.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: many `ftCo_804D8D*`/`ftCo_804D8E*` sdata2 symbols renamed to local `@NNN` labels
raw_diff: `ft_804D6580` and `ft_804D6588` sizes changed from 0x8 to 0x4

key_files.role: Build config updated to list the new matching objects.
Fighter common type surface updated for moved capture/down-reflect state fields.

key_files.path: src/melee/ft/ft_0819.c, src/melee/ft/ft_0C88.c, src/melee/ft/ft_0C8C.c, src/melee/ft/ftcpuattack.c, src/melee/ft/ftdemo.c, src/melee/ft/ftmaterial.c, src/melee/ft/ftmotionstates.c, src/melee/ft/fighter.c, src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage.c, src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DownBound.c, src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DownDamage.c, src/melee/ft/chara/ftGigaKoopa/ftGk_Init.c, src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.c, src/melee/ft/chara/ftLuigi/ftLg_Init.c, src/melee/ft/chara/ftMario/ftMr_Init.c, src/melee/it/items/itleadead.c, src/melee/it/items/itleadead.h, src/melee/it/items/itlikelike.c

evidence_refs: changed_files: listed dependent fighter and item files with small include/prototype/call-site edits
raw_diff: examples include replacing `ft/ft_0C31.h` with split headers such as `ft/ftCo_800C703C.h`, `ft/ftCo_800C7434.h`, and `ft/ftCo_800C7CA0.h`

what_changed: Split the address range 0x800C703C-0x800C8170 out of `ft_0C31.c` into ten new `src/melee/ft/ftCo_800C*.c` files with matching `.h` files.
Updated `config/GALE01/splits.txt` so `ft_0C31.c` now ends at 0x800C703C and the new units own consecutive text/sdata2 ranges through 0x800C8170.
Updated `configure.py` to add the ten new matching objects.
Updated `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` for split-local literals/labels and for sbss object size/ownership around `ft_804D6580` and `ft_804D6588`.
Moved or exposed prototypes for capture, down-reflect, and helper functions into new headers and changed dependent fighter/item files to include those headers.
Bot report recorded +2192 matched code bytes, +8128 linked code bytes, +152 matched data bytes, and 55 new matches; it also reported moved symbols as 36 broken matches in old `main/melee/ft/ft_0C31`.

smart_moves.text: The PR kept the split mechanically consistent by updating source files, per-unit headers, `splits.txt`, `symbols.txt`, and `configure.py` together.
evidence_refs: changed_files: 45 changed files across source, headers, config, and build config
raw_diff: new split ranges plus configure Object(Matching) entries

smart_moves.text: Header names and guards follow the new unit start address, making moved prototypes searchable by address while leaving unknown callbacks as `fn_800C....`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: headers use guards like `GALE01_800C7CA0` and address comments like `/* 7DC4 */ void fn_800C7DC4(...)`

smart_moves.text: The code uses real fighter/item fields for recovered capture state where visible, such as `mv.co.captureleadead`, `mv.co.capturelikelike`, and `mv.co.downreflect`, rather than raw byte offsets.
The bot report makes the TU split easy to audit: new matches appear under the new units and corresponding old-unit matches appear as broken in `ft_0C31`.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists 55 new matches and 36 broken matches

decomp_lessons.lesson: For a translation-unit split, a bot report may show old-unit matches as broken while the same code reappears as new matches in the new units; judge the split using the full report and the split/config updates, not the broken-match list alone.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: broken matches in `main/melee/ft/ft_0C31` for the same symbol names
raw_diff: `splits.txt` moves the range out of `ft_0C31.c`

decomp_lessons.lesson: When extracting functions into new files, update owning headers and downstream includes at the same time so call sites reference the split unit rather than the old aggregate header.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: dependent files add split headers such as `ftCo_800C7434.h`, `ftCo_800C7220.h`, `ftCo_800C7CA0.h`

decomp_lessons.lesson: Split work can require symbol metadata cleanup for local literals and section ownership; this PR updated `symbols.txt` and `splits.txt` together rather than compensating only in C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: sdata2 `ftCo_804D8D*`/`ftCo_804D8E*` names changed to local `@NNN` symbols
raw_diff: `splits.txt` assigns sdata2 ranges to each new split unit

naming_conventions.convention: New split source/header files are named by the first function address, e.g. `ftCo_800C703C.c` and `ftCo_800C7CA0.h`.
Header guards use `GALE01_` plus the unit start address, such as `GALE01_800C703C` and `GALE01_800C7CA0`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: new headers show `#ifndef GALE01_800C703C` and `#ifndef GALE01_800C7CA0`

naming_conventions.convention: Prototype comments retain low address suffixes, such as `/* 7CA0 */ bool ftCo_800C7CA0(...)`, improving search by address.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: new headers contain address comments before prototypes

naming_conventions.convention: Known gameplay state names are used where supported by existing names, e.g. `ftCo_CaptureLeadead_Anim`, `ftCo_CaptureLikelike_Anim`, and `ftCo_DownReflect_Coll`; unresolved callbacks keep address-style `fn_800C....` names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: headers and source mix semantic capture/down-reflect names with `fn_800C74CC`, `fn_800C7568`, `fn_800C7DC4`

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Several new units preserve a three-level inline wrapper chain (`inlineB0`, `inlineB1`, `inlineB2`) that delegates to `ftCo_800C7070`; likely a codegen-shape tactic, but no review explanation is present.
`ftCo_800C7CA0.c` uses `PAD_STACK(8)` and `PAD_STACK(4)` around the down-reflect logic.
`fn_800C7DC4` keeps a goto-based branch section with an explicit `@todo fix branch structure`, indicating an unresolved control-flow shape retained for matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `// @todo fix branch structure` followed by labels `t1`, `t2`, and `end`

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: `fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate` preserves `jobj.h` assert file/line/condition text while routing the dirty-matrix call through a fighter-local helper; the PR evidence is code/comment only, not reviewer endorsement.
Temporary source-local prototypes were used in new C files for cross-split callbacks, e.g. `fn_800C74CC`, `fn_800C7568`, and `fn_800C7890`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `ftCo_800C7590.c` declares `/* 0C74CC */ void fn_800C74CC(...)` and `/* 0C7568 */ void fn_800C7568(...)`

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: `ft_800C80A4` was expressed as a natural `for` loop over fighter parts plus a `while` loop over `HSD_DObj` siblings.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `for (i = idx = 0; i < ftPartsTable[fp->kind]->parts_num; idx++, i++)` and `while (dobj != NULL)` in `ft_800C80A4`

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Automated report showed progress: matched code 68.47% (+0.06%, +2192 bytes), linked code 40.46% (+0.21%, +8128 bytes), matched data 39.77% (+0.01%, +152 bytes), linked data 33.51% (+0.02%, +184 bytes), with 55 new matches.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: The same report listed 36 broken matches, mostly under old unit `main/melee/ft/ft_0C31`, consistent with moved functions after the split.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: broken matches table lists `ft_800C80A4`, `ftCo_800C7800`, `ftCo_800C70D0`, and other moved symbols under `ft_0C31`

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for cleanup of `fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate` or replacement with a shared JObj inline/helper.
Search later PRs for removal of `PAD_STACK` in `ftCo_800C7CA0.c` or cleanup of `fn_800C7DC4` branch structure.
Search later PRs for semantic naming of `fn_800C74CC`, `fn_800C7568`, `fn_800C7890`, and `fn_800C7DC4`.
Search later PRs for prototype movement out of source-local declarations in `ftCo_800C7590.c` and `ftCo_800C78B0.c`.
Search later PRs touching `ft_804D6580`, `ft_804D6588`, and `ft_0C88.c` sbss ownership.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata, changed-file counts, merge time, author, and file list
notes: PR body is empty and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.
The raw diff is truncated in the loaded context, but targeted local reads covered the main new source sections and split/build metadata.
No local build, objdiff, or manual reviewer validation beyond the bot report is present in the slice.
Some tactics are visible in source (`PAD_STACK`, inline wrappers, gotos, source-local prototypes), but the slice does not explain whether each was required or temporary.

accepted_candidate_records.title: TU split reports can show old-unit broken matches and new-unit matches simultaneously
accepted_candidate_records.text: When a PR splits functions out of a translation unit, the automated report may list the moved functions as broken in the old unit while listing them as new matches in the new units. For intake/curation, compare symbol names, split ranges, and configure entries before treating the broken-match list as a regression.
evidence_refs: pr-2527 text_corpus: 55 new matches in `ftCo_800C*` units
pr-2527 text_corpus: broken matches in old `ft_0C31` unit
pr-2527 raw_diff: `splits.txt` and `configure.py` add the new units

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep split metadata, build objects, headers, and call-site includes synchronized
accepted_candidate_records.text: A clean function extraction/split should update `splits.txt`, `configure.py`, owning headers, and dependent includes together, otherwise the move is difficult to audit and can strand prototypes in the old aggregate header.
evidence_refs: pr-2527 changed_files: new source/header files plus config/build/call-site edits
pr-2527 raw_diff: configure adds ten `Object(Matching, ...)` entries
pr-2527 raw_diff: dependent files include new split headers

source_update_candidates.title: Header inline exception: local helper call with original header assert identity
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2527 contains a commented `fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate` that preserves `jobj.h` assert file/line/condition identity while routing the dirty-matrix call through a fighter-local helper. Curator should decide whether this is a narrow accepted exception under header-inlines/assert standards or only a temporary tactic needing later cleanup.
source_update_candidates.reason: Current standards prefer mapping expanded header asserts back to existing helpers/macros; this PR shows a special local-call shape but no review discussion.

source_update_candidates.title: TU split bot-report interpretation
source_update_candidates.text: For large TU splits, document that old-unit broken matches can be expected if the same symbols become new matches in newly listed objects and the split ranges/config entries support the move.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists both new matches under `ftCo_800C*` and broken matches under `ft_0C31`
raw_diff: `splits.txt` and `configure.py` create new object boundaries
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a reusable interpretation rule for curation, but it should be reviewed before being promoted as a global standard.

source_update_candidates.title: `ft_0C31` tail split into `ftCo_800C703C` through `ftCo_800C7CA0`
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2527 moved the 0x800C703C-0x800C8170 range from `src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.c` into ten separate files named by starting address: `ftCo_800C703C.c`, `ftCo_800C7070.c`, `ftCo_800C70D0.c`, `ftCo_800C7178.c`, `ftCo_800C7220.c`, `ftCo_800C739C.c`, `ftCo_800C7434.c`, `ftCo_800C7590.c`, `ftCo_800C78B0.c`, and `ftCo_800C7CA0.c`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `splits.txt` new ranges for the ten files
changed_files: ten new `ftCo_800C*.c` files and headers
source_update_candidates.reason: Concrete path ownership fact supported by split metadata, routed for curator review.

rejection_notes: Do not infer human reviewer approval or final style endorsement for `PAD_STACK`, inline wrapper chains, goto branch shape, or source-local prototypes; the slice has no human review comments.
Do not claim the PR fully matched every touched function solely from the summary; the loaded bot report lists 55 new matches but is truncated after the first 30 plus '25 more'.
Do not promote the raw `__assert` form in `fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate` as a general assert style; it may be a narrow matching exception.
